title: CIL Industrial Engineering — Full-length Mock 03
pattern: CIL MT — 200 MCQ · 2 papers · 1 mark each · no negative marking

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: team / successfully / the / completed / project / the
options: Successfully the team completed the project
The team successfully completed the project
The project successfully completed the team
Completed the project the team successfully
The team successfully completed the project
questions.solution: The team successfully completed the project

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: He is one of those who has excelled in the field.
options: He is one of those who have excelled in the field.
He is one who has excelled in the field.
He is one of those who excelled in the field.
He is one of those who has excelled in the field.
questions.solution: He is one of those who have excelled in the field.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: automation / loss / leads / to / jobs / of
options: Leads to loss of jobs automation
Loss of jobs automation leads to
Automation leads to loss of jobs
Jobs loss of to leads automation
Automation leads to loss of jobs
questions.solution: Automation leads to loss of jobs

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: The informations provided is accurate.
options: The information provided has been accurate.
questions.solution: The information provided is accurate.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: is / of / coal / formation / a / process / slow
options: Coal formation is a slow process
A slow process is formation of coal
Formation of coal is a slow process
Coal formation is a slow process
Coal is a slow process of formation
questions.solution: Coal formation is a slow process

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: The professor gave a one-hour lecture.
options: The professor gave a one hour lecture.
The professor gave a one-hour lecture.
questions.solution: The professor gave a one-hour lecture.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: globalisation / economies / has / world / around / the / transformed
options: Around the world globalisation has transformed economies
Globalisation has transformed economies around the world
Globalisation has transformed economies around the world
Transformed economies globalisation has around the world
Economies around the world has globalisation transformed
questions.solution: Globalisation has transformed economies around the world

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: The police has arrested the suspect last night.
options: The police have arrested the suspect last night.
The police arrested the suspect last night.
The police has arrested the suspect last night.
The police had arrested the suspect last night.
questions.solution: The police arrested the suspect last night.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: workers / demanding / are / wages / better / for
options: Workers are demanding for better wages
For better wages workers are demanding
Demanding are workers better for wages
Better wages for workers are demanding
Workers are demanding for better wages
questions.solution: Workers are demanding for better wages

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: The committee were divided in their opinions.
options: The committee has divided in their opinions.
The committee was divided in their opinions.
The committee is divided in its opinions.
The committee were divided in their opinions.
questions.solution: The committee were divided in their opinions.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: innovation / for / key / is / growth / economic
options: Innovation is key for economic growth
Innovation is key for economic growth
Economic growth is key for innovation
For economic growth innovation is key
Key for growth economic is innovation
questions.solution: Innovation is key for economic growth

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: She not only lost her wallet but also her phone.
options: She not only lost her wallet but also lost.
She lost not only her wallet but also.
She not only lost her wallet but also her phone.
She lost not only her wallet but also her phone.
questions.solution: She lost not only her wallet but also her phone.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: research / this / significant / has / implications / study / for
options: This study has significant implications for research
This study has significant implications for research
Significant implications this study has for research
Research this study has significant implications for
For research this study has significant implications
questions.solution: This study has significant implications for research

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: The number of accidents have increased.
options: A number of accidents has increased.
The number of accidents has increased.
The number of accidents is increasing.
The number of accidents have increased.
questions.solution: The number of accidents has increased.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: solve / team / the / to / problem / together / worked
options: The team worked together to solve the problem
Worked together the team to solve the problem
Together the team worked to solve the problem
The team worked together to solve the problem
To solve the problem the team together worked
questions.solution: The team worked together to solve the problem

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: He asked me to convey him my regards.
options: He asked that I convey him my regards.
He asked me to convey to him my regards.
He asked me to convey my regards to him.
He asked me to convey him my regards.
questions.solution: He asked me to convey to him my regards.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: project / the / completed / was / time / on
options: The project was completed on time
Was the project completed on time
Completed was the project on time
The project was completed on time
On time the project was completed
questions.solution: The project was completed on time

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: One of the miners (A) / have been (B) / hospitalised after (C) / the accident. (D)
questions.solution: B - should be 'has been'

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The government must ____ steps to control pollution.
questions.solution: take

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: She was so tired ____ she fell asleep immediately.
questions.solution: that

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of JEOPARDIZE:
questions.solution: Protect

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: Neither the manager (A) / nor his assistants (B) / was present (C) / at the meeting. (D)
questions.solution: C - should be 'were present'

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of EPHEMERAL:
questions.solution: Eternal

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: He is one of (A) / those who (B) / has excelled (C) / in the field. (D)
questions.solution: C - should be 'have excelled'

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of CONCISE:
questions.solution: Brief

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: 5S: Sort, Set in order, Shine, Standardize, Sustain:
questions.solution: 5S: Japanese workplace organization. Reduces waste, improves safety, increases efficiency.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: VE — creative phase techniques:
options: Brainstorming (Osborn), morphological analysis, TRIZ, analogies. Quantity → quality. Defer judgment
questions.solution: Creative: deferral of judgment, freewheeling, hitchhiking. 200 ideas per session typical.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Sensitivity analysis in LP (shadow price):
options: Dual variable = change in objective per unit increase in RHS. Valid range: allowable increase/decrease
questions.solution: Shadow price: binding constraint has non-zero. Non-binding (slack>0): shadow price = 0. Dual simplex for changes.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: AS/RS (Automated Storage and Retrieval System):
options: High-density storage. Crane or shuttle-based. Dual-command cycle for efficiency
questions.solution: AS/RS: miniload, unit-load, carousel. High-bay warehouse. Dual command: store + retrieve per cycle.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Quality Function Deployment (QFD) House of Quality translates:
questions.solution: QFD: voice of customer → engineering characteristics. Matrix shows relationships. Roof shows trade-offs.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For the CPM network shown (path 1-2-4 = A+B = 5+8, path 1-3-4 = C+D = 2+10), the project duration (critical path) is:
questions.solution: Path durations: 1-2-4 = 13, 1-3-4 = 12. The critical path is the longest = 13 days.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: NIOSH lifting equation: RWL (Recommended Weight Limit) depends on:
options: Horizontal distance, vertical height, frequency, coupling
questions.solution: RWL = LC × HM × VM × DM × AM × FM × CM. Each factor modifies load constant based on conditions.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Theil's U statistic:
questions.solution: Theil's U: measure of forecasting accuracy. U = 1: no better than naive. U = 0: perfect.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An assembly line has a cycle time of 4 min and runs for 420 min/shift. The shift output is:
questions.solution: Output = available time / cycle time = 420/4 = 105 units.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Value Engineering: function analysis defines function as:
questions.solution: VE: active verb + measurable noun. E.g., 'transmit electricity', 'hold weight'. Enables creative alternatives.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Transportation modes:
options: Road (flexible), Rail (bulk, long distance), Air (fast, expensive), Water (slow, cheap), Pipeline (continuous)
questions.solution: Modal choice: cost × speed × reliability × capacity. Intermodal: container (ISO). Multimodal.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Throughput accounting: focuses on:
questions.solution: Throughput = sales - totally variable costs. Inventory = money tied up. OE = money to run. Maximize throughput.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Goal programming preemptive method:
options: Optimizes goals sequentially in priority order
questions.solution: Preemptive: strict priority hierarchy. Higher priority satisfied first.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Economic Order Quantity with quantity discount:
options: Compare total cost at each price break
questions.solution: Quantity discount: total cost curve shifts at each break. Compare TC at EOQ and each break point. Choose minimum.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Gage R&R — measurement system analysis:
options: Repeatability (same operator, same gage) + reproducibility (different operators). %R&R < 10% = acceptable, >30% = unacceptable
questions.solution: GRR: ANOVA method. Variance components: part, operator, interaction, gage. Number of distinct categories > 5.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Fixed-position layout: product remains stationary, resources move to it:
options: Used for large, heavy products (ships, buildings, aircraft)
questions.solution: Fixed position: cranes, workers, materials brought to site. Large-scale construction.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Control chart: Western Electric rules detect:
questions.solution: WE rules: 1 point beyond 3σ, 2/3 beyond 2σ same side, 4/5 beyond 1σ same side, 8 consecutive same side.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Spare parts: critical spares classification:
options: A = essential (stockout stops production). B = important. C = convenience
questions.solution: Critical: keep in stock. Important: source quickly. Convenience: order as needed.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Time-phased order point (TOP) combines:
questions.solution: TOP: orders placed at fixed future dates. Combines EOQ logic with MRP time-phasing. Reduces inventory vs pure EOQ.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The last three demands were 120, 130 and 140 units. The 3-period moving-average forecast is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Automated storage/retrieval (AS/RS):
options: Unit-load, mini-load, micro-load. S/R machine. Dual command cycle efficiency. FIFO, LIFO
questions.solution: AS/RS: high-bay warehouse. Throughput = dual commands/hr. Crane utilization > 90%. Vertical storage.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For D = 3600/yr, S = ₹50, H = ₹4/unit/yr, the minimum total ordering-plus-holding cost (at EOQ ≈ 300) is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Component A (R = 0.9) is in series with a parallel pair B (0.8) and C (0.9). The system reliability is about:

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Human information processing stages:
options: Sensory register then Working memory then Long-term memory
questions.solution: HIP: input to sensory store to working store to decision to response.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Value engineering definition:
options: Systematic function analysis to achieve required function at lowest cost without sacrificing quality
questions.solution: Value = Function/Cost. VE: during design. VA: after product exists.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Learning curve states:
options: Unit time decreases fixed percentage each output doubling
questions.solution: Learning curve: Y = aX^b.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP):
options: Integrated system across functions (finance, HR, supply chain, production)
questions.solution: ERP: SAP, Oracle. Single database. Real-time visibility. Modifications costly.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: MTBF=200h, MTTR=20h. Availability =

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For the CPM network shown (path 1-2-4 = A+B = 4+8, path 1-3-4 = C+D = 2+10), the project duration (critical path) is:
questions.solution: Path durations: 1-2-4 = 12, 1-3-4 = 12. The critical path is the longest = 12 days.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A product is used at 20 units/day with a lead time of 7 days. The reorder point (no safety stock) is:
questions.solution: ROP = demand rate × lead time = 20×7 = 140 units.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Two components of reliability 0.9 and 0.9 are in series. The system reliability is:
questions.solution: Series: Rs = R₁×R₂ = 0.9×0.9 = 0.81.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An assembly line has total task time 48 min, 5 stations and a cycle time of 12 min. Its balance efficiency is:
questions.solution: Efficiency = Σt/(N·C) = 48/(5×12) = 80%.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Value Stream Mapping (VSM) identifies:
options: Value-added and non-value-added activities in a process
questions.solution: VSM: current state → future state. Map material and information flow. Identify waste. Prioritize improvements.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: JIT vs MRP key difference:
questions.solution: JIT: pull from demand. MRP: push from forecast.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Industrial engineering integrates:
questions.solution: IE: optimize complex processes.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Kaizen events are:
questions.solution: Kaizen event: 3-5 days, cross-functional team.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Ergonomics definition:
options: Fit job to worker (not worker to job)
questions.solution: Ergonomics: design work to fit human capabilities.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Ergonomic risk factors include:
questions.solution: Ergonomic risk: multiple factors. Force, repetition, awkward posture, vibration, temperature extremes.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Heijunka (production leveling) aims to:
options: Smooth production volume and mix over time
questions.solution: Heijunka: level production by volume and mix. Reduces Mura (unevenness). Enables Takt time production.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: SPC — rational subgrouping:
options: Subgroup = homogeneous conditions. Instant of time vs consecutive units
questions.solution: Rational subgrouping: minimize within-group variation, maximize between-group variation.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Predictive maintenance — vibration analysis:
options: FFT spectrum. Identify bearing faults, imbalance, misalignment, looseness
questions.solution: Vibration: accelerometers. ISO 10816 severity chart. Enveloping for bearing faults. Order tracking.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Line balancing heuristics:
options: LPT (longest processing time first). RPW (ranked positional weight)
questions.solution: LPT: assign longest remaining task to station. RPW: sum of task + successors' times.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: With α = 0.3, last actual = 300 and last forecast = 280, the next exponential-smoothing forecast is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Containerization — intermodal:
options: ISO containers (20' TEU, 40' FEU). Stackable, modular. Rail/truck/ship. Port terminal efficiency
questions.solution: Container: 20' × 8' × 8'6". Reefers (temp-controlled), tank containers. TEU = 20' equivalent.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Daily demand = 40, lead time = 4 days, safety stock = 80 units. The reorder point is:
questions.solution: ROP = (demand × lead time) + safety stock = 40×4 + 80 = 240 units.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Gantt chart vs network diagram:
options: Gantt: shows schedule, easy to read. Network: shows dependencies
questions.solution: Gantt: horizontal bars for tasks. Network (AON/AOA): precedence relationships.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: JIT/TPS — heijunka (level scheduling):
options: Level production volume and mix. Reduce batch sizes. Smoothed schedule for suppliers
questions.solution: Heijunka: leveling box. Cycle through models. Reduces peak demand on resources.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Goal programming: pre-emptive (lexicographic) method:
options: Priority level P₁ >> P₂ >> P₃. Minimize deviations level by level
questions.solution: Lexicographic: optimize P₁ first. Then P₂ with P₁ fixed. Then P₃ with P₁,P₂ fixed.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Allowances:
options: Personal (5%), fatigue (variable), delay (contingency). Added to normal time
questions.solution: Relaxation allowance: +10-15% for repetitive light work, +20-30% for heavy work.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Break-even: FC=$15000, SP=$45, VC=$15. BEP=

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Measurement system analysis — Gage R&R:
options: Repeatability (same operator, same part) + Reproducibility (different operators). %GRR <10% = good
questions.solution: GRR: ANOVA method. %GRR = σ_GRR/σ_total × 100. ndc = number of distinct categories.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Reliability of parallel (redundant) system:
questions.solution: Parallel: n units. Requires n-fold redundancy. Active vs standby redundancy.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: With demand 4000/yr, order cost ₹40 and order quantity 200, the annual ordering cost is:
questions.solution: Annual ordering cost = (D/Q)×S = (4000/200)×40 = ₹800.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: MTM-1 basic motions: reach (R), move (M), turn (T), grasp (G), position (P), release (RL)
options: Six basic motions: R, M, T, G, P, RL
questions.solution: MTM: Methods-Time Measurement. Each motion has predetermined time based on conditions and distance.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: On the inventory cost curve shown (D = 4000, S = ₹40, H = ₹4), the order size Q* that minimizes total cost (where ordering and holding costs are equal) is:
questions.solution: Total cost is minimum at the EOQ Q* = √(2DS/H) = √(2×4000×40/4) ≈ 282.8 units, where the two cost curves cross.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Cyclegraph / chronocyclegraph:
options: Light traces of worker movements. 3D path recording. Flash rate for velocity
questions.solution: Cyclegraph: continuous path. Chronocyclegraph: interrupted (dash) for speed/direction.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Annual demand = 2000 units, ordering cost = ₹50/order, holding cost = ₹5/unit/yr. The EOQ is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A line makes 400 units using 80 labour-hours. The labour productivity is:
questions.solution: Productivity = output/input = 400/80 = 5 units/hour.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: On the inventory cost curve shown (D = 4000, S = ₹40, H = ₹5), the order size Q* that minimizes total cost (where ordering and holding costs are equal) is:
questions.solution: Total cost is minimum at the EOQ Q* = √(2DS/H) = √(2×4000×40/5) ≈ 253 units, where the two cost curves cross.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Workplace design — anthropometry:
options: Design for 5th to 95th percentile. Adjustable equipment. Reach, clearance, posture
questions.solution: Anthropometry: body dimensions. Percentile design: extreme users (5-95%). Adjustability for all.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Daily demand = 80, lead time = 5 days, safety stock = 80 units. The reorder point is:
questions.solution: ROP = (demand × lead time) + safety stock = 80×5 + 80 = 480 units.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Work measurement techniques:
options: Time study (stopwatch), PMTS (MTM, MOST), Work sampling, Analytical estimation
questions.solution: Time study: direct observation. PMTS: predetermined times. Work sampling: statistical.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Customers arrive at 15/hour and spend on average 1 hour in the system. The average number in the system is:
questions.solution: Little's law: L = λ·W = 15×1 = 15.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Make-to-stock (MTS):
options: Produce before demand. FG inventory. Short delivery. Forecasting essential
questions.solution: MTS: high volume, standard products. Risk of obsolescence. Forecast accuracy critical.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The variance of a PERT activity time is:
questions.solution: Activity variance σ² = ((tp − to)/6)²; path variance is the sum along the critical path.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Goal programming:
options: Multiple objectives. Deviational variables (d+, d-). Prioritize (lexicographic) or weights
questions.solution: Goal: target values. Lexicographic (preemptive priority). Archimedean (weights). GP for soft constraints.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Daily demand = 40, lead time = 4 days, safety stock = 50 units. The reorder point is:
questions.solution: ROP = (demand × lead time) + safety stock = 40×4 + 50 = 210 units.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Interior point methods:
options: Karmarkar (1984). Polynomial time. Follow central path. Barrier function. For large LP
questions.solution: IP: Newton steps. Primal-dual IP. Barrier parameter μ → 0. Often faster than simplex for large problems.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An R chart monitors the process:
questions.solution: The R chart tracks dispersion via the sample range, complementing the X̄ chart.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The last three demands were 140, 110 and 120 units. The 3-period moving-average forecast is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An activity's normal time/cost are 10 days/₹4000 and crash time/cost are 4 days/₹8000. Its crash cost slope is:
questions.solution: Cost slope = (crash − normal cost)/(normal − crash time) = (8000−4000)/(10−4) = ₹666.7/day.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A line makes 800 units using 80 labour-hours. The labour productivity is:
questions.solution: Productivity = output/input = 800/80 = 10 units/hour.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: PERT — Program Evaluation Review Technique:
options: Network analysis with probabilistic time estimates (optimistic, most likely, pessimistic)
questions.solution: PERT: β-distribution. t_e = (a+4m+b)/6. Variance: σ² = ((b-a)/6)². Critical path.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For the CPM network shown (path 1-2-4 = A+B = 3+6, path 1-3-4 = C+D = 2+5), the project duration (critical path) is:
questions.solution: Path durations: 1-2-4 = 9, 1-3-4 = 7. The critical path is the longest = 9 days.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: DMAIC — Measure phase tools:
options: Data collection plan, measurement system analysis, baseline capability, process map
questions.solution: Measure: current state sigma level. Data types: continuous (variable) vs discrete (attribute).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: ISO 9000 series:
options: Quality management standards. ISO 9001:2015 — requirements. Process approach
questions.solution: ISO: 7 principles (customer focus, leadership, engagement, process, improvement, evidence, relationship).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A process has USL = 112, LSL = 88, mean = 100, σ = 2. Its Cpk is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: DMAIC — Analyze phase tools:
options: Fishbone, FMEA, hypothesis testing, regression, ANOVA, multi-vari chart
questions.solution: Analyze: root cause identification. 5 Whys. Hypothesis test: t-test, F-test, chi-square.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: FMEA: Risk Priority Number (RPN) = Severity × Occurrence × Detection. Max RPN:
questions.solution: RPN: S×O×D. Each 1-10. High RPN: priority for corrective action. RPN > 100 typically flagged.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Design of Experiments: factorial design advantage:
options: Studies main effects AND interactions between factors
questions.solution: Full factorial: 2^k experiments for k factors. Interactions reveal how factors work together.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Dynamic programming Bellman equation:
options: Optimal value of current state in terms of future states
questions.solution: V(s) = min_a [c(s,a) + V(s_prime)]. Principle of optimality.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a process (functional) layout, similar machines are grouped:
questions.solution: Process layout suits low-volume, high-variety (job-shop) production.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: VE: function definition format is verb + noun:
options: Examples: transmit electricity, store energy, support load
questions.solution: Function: action + object. Measurable. Example: convert energy, transmit signal, support weight.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Fixed cost = ₹50000, price = ₹50/unit, variable cost = ₹60/unit. The break-even quantity is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Queuing — M/G/1:
options: General service distribution. Pollaczek-Khinchin formula. L = λ·E[S] + λ²·E[S²]/(2(1-ρ))
questions.solution: P-K: L = ρ + (λ²·σ²+ρ²)/(2(1-ρ)). For M/M/1: E[S²] = 2/μ² (exponential).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: MRP lot sizing: Silver-Meal heuristic:
options: Minimize average cost per period by varying lot size (dynamic)
questions.solution: Silver-Meal: find period T where cumulative cost/T is minimized. Order covers periods 1 through T.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Slotting SKU affinity — association mining:
options: Apriori algorithm for items frequently ordered together. Position near each other in warehouse
questions.solution: Association: rules (A→B). Support (frequency), confidence (conditional probability). Pick correlation.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A process has USL = 110, LSL = 88, mean = 102, σ = 3. Its Cpk is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The last three demands were 100, 110 and 120 units. The 3-period moving-average forecast is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Reverse logistics:
options: Returns, recycling, remanufacturing, disposal. Closed-loop supply chain
questions.solution: Reverse: product take-back. Value recovery. Remanufacturing: like-new condition (automotive parts).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Single-period model (newsvendor):
options: Service level = Cu/(Cu+Co). Cu = underage cost, Co = overage cost
questions.solution: Newsvendor: balance risk of overstocking vs understocking. Perishable goods, seasonal items.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An activity's normal time/cost are 10 days/₹3000 and crash time/cost are 4 days/₹5000. Its crash cost slope is:
questions.solution: Cost slope = (crash − normal cost)/(normal − crash time) = (5000−3000)/(10−4) = ₹333.3/day.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Availability = MTBF/(MTBF+MTTR). For MTBF=100h, MTTR=10h:
questions.solution: Availability: proportion of time system is operational. Higher MTBF or lower MTTR improves availability.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Reliability-Centered Maintenance (RCM):
options: Failure mode analysis → maintenance task selection. 7 questions: functions, failures, effects, consequences, tasks
questions.solution: RCM: MSG-3 (aviation). Hidden vs evident failures. Safety, environmental, operational, non-operational.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: VE evaluation phase — weighted matrix:
options: Weighted criteria × score = total. Compare alternatives against functions. Matrix shows best value option
questions.solution: Evaluation: criteria weighted (1-10). Score: 1-10 per alternative. Total = Σ(w_i × s_i).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Neural network forecasting (NNAR):
options: NNAR(p,k): p = lags, k = hidden. One-step ahead prediction. Non-linear. No interpretable parameters
questions.solution: NNAR: feedforward. ReLu/sigmoid activation. Ensemble (multiple random starts). Not extrapolate trend well.
